data.content:
The build generates the file successfully but never adds it to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` items inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the `.cs` file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The compiler doesn't know about the file because it was generated after the `Compile` items were already collected.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target, after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generated the `.cs` file but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects `Compile` items before `CoreCompile` runs, so a dynamically created file must be explicitly included.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is picked up by the compiler.
